schema.name: Wakr Data Lake
schema.description: Star-schema data lake for Wakr market intelligence platform. Tracks towboat/watersport boat listings, pricing, inventory velocity, dealer performance, and member activity across national dealer networks.
changelog: v1.8.0 (2026-05-14): mart_daily_snapshot promoted to central analytics workhorse. (1) mart_daily_snapshot fully specified: grain is manufacturer_key / boat_model_key / state / inventory_type / date_key. Every dashboard metric stored as a column on this row. All parameter combinations (make, model, state, inventory_type, time range) are resolved at query time via WHERE/GROUP BY on this mart — no pre-stored permutations. (2) Metric columns classified as STOCK (point-in-time — use latest row in window or AVG; do NOT SUM across days) vs FLOW (daily count — safe to SUM across days). Classification documented per column. (3) All other Phase-1 marts (mart_inventory_summary, mart_pricing_trends, mart_regional_summary, mart_time_on_market, mart_estimated_velocity) slimmed to monthly-grain only, retaining only metrics that genuinely cannot be derived from mart_daily_snapshot aggregation: MoM comparisons, momentum_status classification, and vs_market_pct comparisons. Trailing window metrics (T7, T30, T90, YTD, L12M) removed from monthly marts — all served by mart_daily_snapshot. (4) Refresh schedule documented: nightly ETL triggered by scrape completion. Trailing 30 days is always relative to date of view (current calendar date), requiring nightly mart_daily_snapshot refresh. (5) query_layer_notes section added to schema documenting stock vs flow aggregation rules and parameter resolution patterns.

v1.7.0 (2026-05-14): Full Layer 2 reconciliation against layer2_central_db_schema_v2.sql. (1) layer2_db section rebuilt with all 17 Layer 2 tables, correct columns, and data_lake_projection mappings. (2) Table name corrections: gear_brands (was l2_manufacturer), boats_models (was l2_boat_model), waters, dealer_groups, dealers, dealer_brands. (3) l2 sync columns added/corrected on dim_manufacturer (l2_gear_brand_id), dim_boat_model (l2_boats_model_id), dim_lake (l2_waters_id), dim_dealer_network (l2_dealer_group_id), dim_dealer (l2_dealer_id). (4) dim_lake: avg_depth_ft, salinity added. (5) dim_manufacturer: slug, country_of_origin, is_active added. (6) dim_boat_model: default_engine_model_id (Phase 2 placeholder), is_active added. (7) dim_boat_instance: canonical_boat_id UUID added from dealer_inventories dedup strategy. (8) bridge_dealer_manufacturer.authorization_type corrected to L2 lowercase values. (9) bridge_listing_features: trailer category added. (10) Engine and floorplan layers documented in layer2_db as Phase 2 candidates. (11) source_trust_hierarchy updated to match source_priority_ref exactly.

v1.6.0 (2026-05-14): Time Range parameter gap analysis and Layer 2 DB additions. (1) Confirmed Time Range dropdown values: Last 7 Days | Trailing 30 Days | Last 90 Days | Year to Date | Last 12 Months. (2) Gap analysis: only T30 and MoM were previously supported in mart computed columns; T7, T90, YTD, and L12M had no pre-aggregated support. (3) All Phase-1 marts updated: mart_inventory_summary grain changed to daily to support T7; all marts now carry a supported_time_ranges list and window-parameterized computed column sets for T7, T30, T90, YTD, L12M. Added mart_daily_snapshot as a new lightweight daily-grain mart to power T7 and exact T30 queries without hitting raw facts. (4) Layer 2 DB documented: l2_manufacturer and l2_boat_model tables defined as the authoritative source for dim_manufacturer and dim_boat_model respectively. l2_manufacturer_id and l2_boat_model_id foreign key columns added to corresponding Data Lake dimensions to maintain sync linkage. Model dropdown on all tabs resolves value list from l2_boat_model scoped to selected Make.

v1.5.0 (2026-05-14): Figma dashboard UX v2 audit — Inventory, Regional, and Pricing tabs reviewed (Dealers skipped, not yet implemented). Changes: (1) Model filter added as a first-class parameter across all three tabs — mart grains expanded to include boat_model_key. (2) Comparison % vs market added as a companion computed column to every KPI metric box on all tabs. (3) mart_inventory_summary: added inventory_added_t30, dom_bucket_0_7/8_15/16_30/31_60/60_plus for Days on Market Distribution chart, active_units column, and resolved momentum_status definition (Accelerating | Stable | Slowing) — removed PENDING status. (4) mart_pricing_trends: added avg_list_price, median_list_price, mom_price_change_pct, top_selling_band_low/high/units, avg_dom_by_price_band_* and listing_count_by_price_band_* for all 6 price bands (under_60k, 60_80k, 80_100k, 100_120k, 120_140k, over_140k), and price_band_min/max columns to support Listings by Price Tier chart and Model Price Efficiency table. (5) mart_regional_summary: new mart added to support Regional tab — state-level grain with listings, avg_dom, boats_sold_t30, pct_market, avg_price, yoy_supply_change_pct, fastest/slowest market derivation, and sales_trend_rising/falling state counts. All mart grains and FK references updated accordingly.

v1.4.0 (2026-03-06): Figma dashboard analytics audit. Added dim_brand_tier (price-based segment config table). Added brand_tier_key FK on dim_manufacturer. Added state_name to dim_geography. Added computed metric columns to all Phase-1 mart tables: sell_through_rate, days_supply, mom_listing_change_pct (mart_inventory_summary); pct_listings_with_price_cut, discount_pressure_pct (mart_pricing_trends); dom_status, aging_risk_level (mart_time_on_market); demand_supply_ratio, momentum_status [PENDING] (mart_estimated_velocity); delta_week_units, market_share_pct [PENDING] (mart_dealer_scorecard). territory on dim_dealer intentionally omitted — DMA from dim_geography.dma serves this purpose. Intelligence signals feed intentionally omitted — computed at runtime. price_tier column omitted — not needed as stored field.

v1.3.0 (2026-03-05): Added dim_dealer_network for multi-location dealer group rollup. Replaced single manufacturer_key FK on dim_dealer with bridge_dealer_manufacturer (many-to-many). Renamed dim_boat_model configurable fields to base_* to clarify they represent manufacturer baseline specs, not per-instance configuration.
v1.2.0 (2026-03-04): Initial JSON export of ERD v3. Split dim_boat into dim_boat_model (type/template) and dim_boat_instance (physical unit).
notes: All fact tables carry both boat_key (instance) and boat_model_key (model) FKs to support aggregation at either grain without extra joins.
VIN is nullable on dim_boat_instance — deduplication uses make+model+year+hours+price-tolerance matching across sources.
Phase 2 tables are in development and subject to change with early brand partners.
dim_member uses hashed IDs — no PII in any fact table.
fact_listing_snapshot should be partitioned by date_key (month) and manufacturer prefix on boat_model_key for query performance.
Each physical dealer location is a separate dim_dealer row. Roll up to dealer group via dim_dealer_network.
Dealer-to-manufacturer affiliation is many-to-many via bridge_dealer_manufacturer; a primary flag identifies the dealer's primary brand for denormalization convenience.

dim_boat_model base_* fields (base_engine_hp, base_ballast_lbs, base_tower_included) are manufacturer baseline specs for the model. Actual per-instance values are stored on dim_boat_instance and bridge_listing_features.

dim_brand_tier defines price-based boat segments (Entry/Mid/Premium) via min_price/max_price thresholds. Segment for a given listing is resolved by matching listed_price against these ranges at query time.
territory on dim_dealer is intentionally not stored as a separate column — use dim_geography.dma (Nielsen DMA) as the territory proxy.
demand_supply_ratio = boats_sold_t30 / active_listings. Demand is defined as count of boats sold over the measurement period — no external signal required.

momentum_status definition RESOLVED (v1.5.0): Accelerating = avg_dom trending down MoM AND sell_through_rate trending up; Slowing = avg_dom trending up MoM OR sell_through_rate trending down; Stable = neither threshold met. Thresholds configurable.
market_share_pct definition PENDING user research — reserved placeholder in mart_dealer_scorecard.

Model parameter (v1.5.0): Model values are always scoped to the selected Make. When Make = All Makes, Model filter is disabled. mart grains that include boat_model_key support model-level slicing without additional joins.

Comparison % vs market (v1.5.0): Every KPI metric box on Inventory, Regional, and Pricing tabs shows a companion pct_vs_market column. This is computed as ((user_company_value - market_avg_value) / market_avg_value) with sign indicating above (+) or below (-) market. User company is identified by Make and optionally Model. Stored as a computed column on each mart; positive = user is above market, negative = below.

Time Range parameter (v1.6.0): Five windows supported — Last 7 Days (T7), Trailing 30 Days (T30), Last 90 Days (T90), Year to Date (YTD), Last 12 Months (L12M). T7 and exact T30 are served by mart_daily_snapshot (daily grain). T90, YTD, and L12M are served by rolling aggregations over monthly mart rows or by mart_daily_snapshot for precision. Monthly marts (pricing, regional, velocity) pre-aggregate at monthly grain; multi-month windows (T90, L12M, YTD) are computed by summing/averaging the appropriate monthly rows at query time. YTD resets on Jan 1 of the current calendar year.

Layer 2 DB (v1.6.0): l2_manufacturer and l2_boat_model are the authoritative source tables maintained in a separate Layer 2 reference DB. dim_manufacturer and dim_boat_model in the Data Lake are downstream projections synced on a daily schedule. Surrogate key mapping preserved via l2_manufacturer_id and l2_boat_model_id columns added to the respective dim tables.

Layer 2 reconciliation (v1.7.0): Layer 2 table names corrected throughout — gear_brands, boats_models, waters, dealer_groups, dealers, dealer_brands. All l2_*_id sync columns updated to reflect actual Layer 2 PK names.

canonical_boat_id (v1.7.0): UUID from dealer_inventories added to dim_boat_instance. Durable cross-source boat identity. HIN is gold-standard dedup signal; source_record_key (URL) is scraper upsert key.

Engine layer (v1.7.0): Layer 2 has engine_brands → engine_models → boat_engine_instances and boats_models.default_engine_model_id. No Data Lake projection yet — Phase 2. default_engine_model_id placeholder added to dim_boat_model.
Floorplan layer (v1.7.0): dealer_floorplan_config + inventory_floorplan + floorplan_curtailment_events documented as Phase 2 candidate for Dealer Financial Health mart.
authorization_type correction (v1.7.0): bridge_dealer_manufacturer values corrected to lowercase L2 values: full | service_only | parts_only | pending | other.
listing_features trailer (v1.7.0): 'trailer' added to bridge_listing_features.feature_category — was missing from prior ERD.
dim_lake additions (v1.7.0): avg_depth_ft and salinity added from waters.
dim_manufacturer additions (v1.7.0): slug, country_of_origin, is_active added from gear_brands.
dim_dealer note (v1.7.0): primary_manufacturer_key has no direct L2 backing — populate from dealer_brands WHERE is_primary=TRUE during sync.
Source priority (v1.7.0): manufacturer_feed(0) < manual_import(1) < directus_cms(2) < scraper(3) < website_cms(4).

mart_daily_snapshot architecture (v1.8.0): Central workhorse mart. Grain = manufacturer_key / boat_model_key / state / inventory_type / date_key. Every dashboard metric is a column. Parameter combinations (make, model, state, inventory_type, time range) are WHERE/GROUP BY filters at query time — never pre-stored permutations. 400-500 parameter combinations cost nothing to add; they are just different queries against the same mart.

Stock vs Flow (v1.8.0): STOCK metrics (active_listings, avg_list_price, median_list_price, avg_dom, dom_bucket_*) are point-in-time — do NOT SUM across days. Use the most recent row in the window for current state, or AVG across the window for period average. FLOW metrics (new_listings, removed_listings) are daily counts — safe to SUM across any window.
Trailing 30 days (v1.8.0): Always relative to the calendar date of the user's view (not scrape date). Requires mart_daily_snapshot to be refreshed nightly. Query: WHERE date_key >= CURRENT_DATE - 30.

Nightly refresh (v1.8.0): mart_daily_snapshot refreshed every night regardless of scrape schedule, to keep trailing window metrics current relative to view date. Full mart recalculation. Scrape-triggered refresh additionally runs when new source data arrives (1-2x per week), updating fact tables and all marts.

Monthly marts (v1.8.0): Slimmed to monthly-grain metrics only — MoM comparisons, momentum_status, vs_market_pct. Trailing window metrics removed. Monthly marts refresh on scrape-triggered schedule only (not nightly), since their inputs (completed calendar months) only change when new scrape data arrives.

layer2_db.description: Wakr Central Business Database (Layer 2). PostgreSQL 16, schema v2.5.0. The authoritative operational database. The Data Lake (Layer 3) dimensions are downstream projections of Layer 2 reference tables, synced on a daily schedule.

source_priority_ref.description: Single source of truth for valid _source values and write-conflict priority. All _source columns on Layer 2 business tables carry an FK to this table. Higher priority integer wins a write conflict.

tables.description: Master manufacturer/brand registry. Single source of truth for all boat make names, partner status, and active flag. Maps to dim_manufacturer in the Data Lake. directus_id is an ETL sync artifact only — not the authoritative row identity.

columns.name: directus_id
columns.notes: ETL sync artifact. NULL until Website ETL pushes row to Directus. Do not use as write or upsert key.

columns.name: external_id
columns.notes: Source system own ID (e.g. manufacturer catalog ID).

columns.name: external_source
columns.notes: FK source_priority_ref.source_name.

columns.name: is_active
columns.notes: FALSE for discontinued or delisted brands.

columns.name: _source
columns.notes: FK source_priority_ref. Row-level write authority.

tables.description: Master boat model registry. One row per make/model/model_year. UNIQUE on (brand_id, name, model_year). default_engine_model_id links to factory-standard engine (FK engine_models.id). Model filter dropdown on all dashboard tabs resolves its value list from this table scoped to selected brand_id.

columns.name: directus_id
columns.notes: ETL sync artifact only.

columns.name: external_source
columns.notes: FK source_priority_ref.

columns.name: slug
columns.notes: Per-brand unique where NOT NULL.

columns.name: model_year
columns.notes: 0 = unknown. Part of UNIQUE (brand_id, name, model_year).

columns.name: boat_type
columns.notes: CHECK: Ski | Wake | Surf | Crossover | Other

columns.name: default_engine_model_id
columns.notes: FK engine_models.id. Factory-standard engine. NULL = not yet normalized or varies by trim.

tables.description: Operational lake/body-of-water reference. Maps to dim_lake. DMA removed — analytics concern computed in Layer 3 from zip/geo data. Includes PostGIS geometry (postgis extension). Unique on (name, state) where both non-null.

columns.name: directus_id
columns.notes: ETL sync artifact only.

columns.name: avg_depth_ft
columns.notes: Not yet in dim_lake — add in next sync pass.

columns.name: salinity
columns.notes: CHECK: fresh | salt | brackish. Not yet in dim_lake — add in next sync pass.

columns.name: lake_type
columns.notes: CHECK: Reservoir | Natural | Private | Other

tables.description: Multi-location dealer networks and broker entities. Maps to dim_dealer_network. Manufacturers are NOT here — gear_brands is the manufacturer table. group_type: dealer_group | broker | other.

columns.name: directus_id
columns.notes: ETL sync artifact only.

columns.name: group_type
columns.notes: CHECK: dealer_group | broker | other

columns.name: network_type
columns.notes: CHECK: franchise | independent | corporate | other. NOTE: ERD used Title Case — L2 uses lowercase.

tables.description: Operational dealer record. One row per physical location. Maps to dim_dealer. primary_brand_id removed from Layer 2 — derive via dealer_brands WHERE is_primary = TRUE. DMA removed. water_id FK to waters (nearest lake) maps to dim_lake via l2_waters_id.

columns.name: directus_id
columns.notes: ETL sync artifact only.

columns.name: dealer_group_id
columns.notes: FK dealer_groups.id. NULL for independent dealers.

columns.name: water_id
columns.notes: FK waters.id. Nearest lake. Maps to dim_lake in Data Lake via l2_waters_id.

tables.description: Many-to-many bridge between dealers and gear_brands. Maps to bridge_dealer_manufacturer. UNIQUE (dealer_id, brand_id). authorization_type uses lowercase values — ERD corrected to match.

columns.name: authorization_type
columns.notes: CHECK: full | service_only | parts_only | pending | other.

tables.description: Core operational listing table. One row per listing per source. Maps to fact_listing_snapshot (via daily scrape) and dim_boat_instance. source_record_key (URL) is the scraper upsert key. canonical_boat_id (UUID) is the durable cross-source boat identity. HIN is the gold-standard dedup signal. listing_status replaces legacy boolean flags: active | draft | sold | inactive | deleted | archived.
tables.dedup_strategy: source_record_key = scraper upsert handle. canonical_boat_id = durable UUID, assigned on first insert, coalesced by dedup worker. HIN takes precedence when present and trusted.
tables.key_columns: id (BIGSERIAL PK), canonical_boat_id (UUID), source_record_key, dealer_id, brand_id, model_id, year, make, model, hin, condition, hours, price, original_list_price, has_tower, actual_ballast_lbs, listing_status, first_seen_at, last_scraped_at

tables.description: One row per feature per listing. Maps to bridge_listing_features. category CHECK values: tower | ballast | electronics | upholstery | engine_upgrade | trailer | other. 'trailer' was missing from prior ERD — corrected v1.7.0.
Engine manufacturer reference (e.g. Indmar, PCM, Ilmor, Volvo Penta). No Data Lake projection — Phase 2.

One row per engine model/config. drive_type: inboard | outboard | sterndrive | jet. fuel_type: gas | diesel | electric | hybrid. Referenced by boats_models.default_engine_model_id and boat_engine_instances. No Data Lake projection — Phase 2.
One row per physical engine per listing (twin-engine = 2 rows). engine_model_id nullable until ETL normalizes. No Data Lake projection — Phase 2.
Floorplan credit agreement terms per dealer (lender, credit line, rate index, curtailment schedule). No Data Lake projection — Phase 2 candidate for Dealer Financial Health mart.
One row per floored unit (1:1 with dealer_inventories). Tracks lender, floored amount, effective rate, manufacturer subsidy window, payoff status. No Data Lake projection — Phase 2.
Time-series log of curtailment events per floored unit. Powers dealer financial risk analytics. No Data Lake projection — Phase 2.
Photo ingestion pipeline tracking (child of dealer_inventories). Not projected into Data Lake.
PostGIS point geometry per listing (WGS-84 SRID 4326). Not directly projected into Data Lake — geo captured via dim_geography at zip/state level.
Unified per-field source tracking ETL audit table. Not projected into Data Lake.
Audit log of source-priority write conflicts. ETL audit table. Not projected into Data Lake.

Minimal identity for private-sale listing posters. directus_id is TEXT/UUID (only L2 table where this is UUID not INTEGER). Not projected into Data Lake — PII-adjacent; member analytics use dim_member with hashed IDs.

sync_notes: Sync key convention: Layer 2 integer PK stored as l2_{tablename}_id on the corresponding Data Lake dimension.
Model filter dropdown resolves value list from boats_models scoped to selected brand_id — not from scraped listing coverage.
authorization_type: Layer 2 lowercase (full | service_only | parts_only | pending | other). Data Lake bridge_dealer_manufacturer corrected to match.
dim_lake is missing avg_depth_ft and salinity — add in next sync pass.
canonical_boat_id (UUID) from dealer_inventories added to dim_boat_instance as durable cross-source identity.
dim_dealer.primary_manufacturer_key has no direct L2 backing — must be populated from dealer_brands WHERE is_primary = TRUE during sync.
Recommended sync frequency: daily for reference tables; near-real-time for is_active and partner status changes.

schema.source_priority_note: Source priority values mirror Layer 2 source_priority_ref table. Higher integer wins write conflict.

query_layer_notes.description: Rules the query layer must follow when reading from mart_daily_snapshot. These are not enforced by the database — they must be implemented in the API or BI layer.

stock_metrics.rule: Do NOT SUM across days. For current state: use MAX(date_key) row in the window. For period average: AVG() across days in the window. For period-end snapshot: use the single row where date_key = last day of window.
stock_metrics.example: Active listings for Malibu 23 LSV FL New T30 = value on the most recent date_key in the window, not the sum of 30 days of active_listing counts.

flow_metrics.rule: Safe to SUM across any window. These are daily counts of events (listings added or removed that day).
flow_metrics.example: Inventory Added T30 = SUM(new_listings) WHERE date_key >= CURRENT_DATE - 30.

parameter_resolution.make_filter: WHERE manufacturer_key = X. When 'All Makes': omit WHERE clause or no filter.
parameter_resolution.model_filter: WHERE boat_model_key = Y. Only valid when a specific Make is selected. When 'All Models': omit.
parameter_resolution.inventory_type_filter: WHERE inventory_type IN ('New', 'Used', 'New','Used'). 'Combined' = no filter (all rows).

query_layer_notes.vs_market_pct: Comparison % (user company vs market) cannot be derived from mart_daily_snapshot alone in a single pass. Requires two aggregations: (1) filtered to user's make/model, (2) unfiltered or filtered to all other makes. Sourced from monthly mart pre-computed columns where available, or computed as two subqueries against mart_daily_snapshot for trailing windows.
query_layer_notes.all_states_rollup: Aggregating 'All States' over a 30-day window on mart_daily_snapshot may touch ~30 days × 50 states × N models rows. For stock metrics use AVG or latest-date value per state then aggregate. Index on (manufacturer_key, boat_model_key, date_key) is critical for performance.
query_layer_notes.boats_sold_proxy: removed_listings is the proxy for boats sold (listing removed after active status = inferred sale). This mirrors fact_estimated_sale logic. For precision, join to fact_estimated_sale filtered by confidence_score threshold.

refresh_schedule.description: Two independent refresh triggers. Nightly refresh keeps trailing window metrics current. Scrape-triggered refresh updates fact data when new source data arrives.

nightly_refresh.trigger: Nightly calendar job — runs regardless of scrape activity
nightly_refresh.time: Recommended: 2-4am local time, after any scrape windows complete
nightly_refresh.reason: Trailing 30 days is relative to CURRENT_DATE (date of user view). Without nightly refresh, T30 on a Wednesday would include data from before the last scrape but the window endpoint would be stale. mart_daily_snapshot must be current-dated every night.
nightly_refresh.what_changes: New row inserted for CURRENT_DATE for every active make/model/state/inventory_type combination. Prior rows are immutable once written — only today's row is added or updated.

scrape_triggered_refresh.trigger: On completion of scrape ETL pipeline — 1-2x per week
sequence: 1. Scrape completes — new records land in Layer 2 dealer_inventories
2. Layer 2 → Data Lake dimension sync (gear_brands → dim_manufacturer, boats_models → dim_boat_model, dealers → dim_dealer, etc.)
3. fact_listing_snapshot rows inserted for new/changed listings
4. fact_estimated_sale rows inserted for listings inferred as sold (removed after active)
5. mart_daily_snapshot rebuilt for the scrape date (backfill if scrape covers multiple days)
6. Monthly marts recalculated: mart_pricing_trends, mart_regional_summary, mart_inventory_summary (monthly grain), mart_time_on_market, mart_estimated_velocity
7. refresh_log row inserted recording scrape date, rows affected, mart refresh timestamps

refresh_schedule.monthly_mart_refresh_note: Monthly marts (mart_pricing_trends, mart_regional_summary, etc.) refresh on scrape-triggered schedule only — not nightly. Their inputs are completed calendar months which do not change between scrapes. The current in-progress month row is updated on each scrape-triggered refresh and flagged is_partial_month = true.

staleness_expectations.mart_daily_snapshot_flow_metrics: Max 3-7 days stale between scrapes — flow metrics (new_listings, removed_listings) only change when new scrape data arrives

dimensions.description: Standard date spine used across all fact tables.
Geographic hierarchy used for regional performance analytics and location-based filtering.

columns.name: state_name
columns.notes: Full state name, e.g. Florida. Required for Regional tab State column display.

columns.name: region
columns.notes: e.g. Southeast, Mountain West

columns.name: dma
columns.notes: Nielsen DMA for media alignment. Also serves as the dealer territory proxy — see dim_dealer notes.

dimensions.description: Data provenance tracking for every fact record. Enables source trust hierarchy filtering.

columns.name: source_name
columns.notes: e.g. BoatTrader, YachtWorld, Dealer Feed, Manufacturer, DTC Member

columns.name: source_type
columns.notes: Scrape | API | Manual | Member

columns.name: scrape_domain
columns.notes: e.g. boattrader.com, yachtworld.com

columns.name: reliability_tier
columns.notes: High | Medium | Low

dimensions.description: Boat manufacturers / brands. Root of the boat type hierarchy. Downstream projection of l2_manufacturer (Layer 2 DB). Synced daily.

foreign_keys.notes: Segment tier for this manufacturer's primary product line. Resolved via dim_brand_tier price ranges.

columns.name: l2_gear_brand_id
columns.notes: FK to gear_brands.id (Layer 2). Maintained by daily sync.

columns.name: brand_tier_key
columns.notes: FK to dim_brand_tier. Replaces the legacy brand_tier VARCHAR field for structured config-driven segmentation.

columns.name: is_wakr_partner
columns.notes: Paying data/dashboard partner

columns.name: slug
columns.notes: From gear_brands.slug. Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: country_of_origin
columns.notes: From gear_brands.country_of_origin. Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: is_active
columns.notes: From gear_brands.is_active. FALSE for discontinued brands. Added v1.7.0.

dimensions.description: Structured configuration table defining price-based boat market segments (e.g. Entry, Mid, Premium). Each row defines a named tier with a price floor and optional ceiling. Segment for any given listing is resolved by matching listed_price (new boat) against min_price/max_price ranges at query time. Powers the Segment Momentum view on the Overview tab and the Avg Days on Market by Price Tier chart on the Pricing tab.

columns.name: tier_name
columns.notes: e.g. Entry | Mid | Premium

columns.name: min_price
columns.notes: Minimum new-boat list price (inclusive) for this segment

columns.name: max_price
columns.notes: Maximum new-boat list price (exclusive). NULL = no upper bound (top-tier segment).

columns.name: display_order
columns.notes: Sort order for dashboard segment display (1 = lowest tier)

dimensions.description: Dealer groups and multi-location networks. A dealer network (e.g. a regional chain or franchise group) may operate many individual dealer locations. Each location is a separate dim_dealer row; this table provides the rollup entity for group-level analytics such as total network inventory, network-wide sell-through, and aggregated dealer scorecards.

columns.name: l2_dealer_group_id
columns.notes: FK to dealer_groups.id (Layer 2). Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: network_name
columns.notes: e.g. 'Lake Powell Marine Group', 'Southeast Malibu Dealers'

columns.name: parent_company
columns.notes: Corporate parent entity if applicable

columns.name: network_type
columns.notes: Franchise | Independent | Corporate Chain

columns.name: is_wakr_partner
columns.notes: Network-level Wakr partnership flag

dimensions.description: Individual boat dealer locations. Each physical location is a separate row. Roll up to dealer group via dealer_network_key → dim_dealer_network. Dealers may represent multiple manufacturers; all brand affiliations are tracked via bridge_dealer_manufacturer. The primary_manufacturer_key is a denormalized convenience FK for the dealer's primary/majority brand.

foreign_keys.notes: Denormalized primary brand; full list via bridge_dealer_manufacturer

columns.name: l2_dealer_id
columns.notes: FK to dealers.id (Layer 2). Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: dealer_id_external
columns.notes: Source system ID

columns.name: primary_manufacturer_key
columns.notes: Primary brand affiliation — full multi-brand list in bridge_dealer_manufacturer

columns.name: is_wakr_partner
columns.notes: Enrolled in Dealer Listings product

dimensions.description: DTC members registered on wakr.co. PII-safe — hashed IDs only.

columns.name: member_id_hashed
columns.notes: SHA-256 hash of source member ID — no PII

columns.name: skill_level
columns.notes: Beginner | Intermediate | Advanced

dimensions.description: Lakes and waterways where towboats are used. Unique to the watersports market — supports usage-pattern analytics.

columns.name: l2_waters_id
columns.notes: FK to waters.id (Layer 2). Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: lake_type
columns.notes: Reservoir | Natural | Private

columns.name: avg_depth_ft
columns.notes: From waters.avg_depth_ft (Layer 2). Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: salinity
columns.notes: CHECK: fresh | salt | brackish. From waters.salinity (Layer 2). Added v1.7.0.

dimensions.description: The TYPE/TEMPLATE dimension — one row per make/model/year combination. Represents the manufacturer's base product specification. base_* fields (base_engine_hp, base_ballast_lbs, base_tower_included) are the factory-standard configuration for the model and should not be confused with actual per-instance values, which are stored on dim_boat_instance and bridge_listing_features. Downstream projection of l2_boat_model (Layer 2 DB), enriched with scrape-derived data. Synced daily. The Model filter dropdown on all dashboard tabs resolves its value list from l2_boat_model (Layer 2), not this table, to ensure completeness independent of scraped listing coverage.

columns.name: l2_boats_model_id
columns.notes: FK to boats_models.id (Layer 2). Maintained by daily sync.

columns.name: boat_type
columns.notes: Ski | Wake | Surf | Crossover

columns.name: hull_length_ft
columns.notes: Model-defining spec from manufacturer

columns.name: engine_type
columns.notes: Engine family/series name — model-defining

columns.name: base_engine_hp
columns.notes: Factory standard HP for this model. Actual instance HP may differ (upgrades) — see dim_boat_instance / bridge_listing_features

columns.name: base_tower_included
columns.notes: True if tower is standard on this model. Actual presence on a specific boat tracked on dim_boat_instance

columns.name: base_ballast_lbs
columns.notes: Factory standard ballast. Actual ballast on a specific boat tracked on dim_boat_instance / bridge_listing_features

columns.name: msrp
columns.notes: From manufacturer feed when available

columns.name: default_engine_model_id
columns.notes: From boats_models.default_engine_model_id → engine_models.id. Phase 2 only — engine dimension not yet built. Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: is_active
columns.notes: From boats_models.is_active. FALSE for discontinued model-years. Added v1.7.0.

dimensions.description: The PHYSICAL UNIT dimension — one row per actual boat (VIN or surrogate). Many instances per model. Stores actual as-delivered/as-listed configuration — use this for per-hull values that may differ from the model baseline (e.g. upgraded engine, added ballast, aftermarket tower).

columns.name: canonical_boat_id
columns.notes: Durable cross-source boat identity UUID from dealer_inventories.canonical_boat_id (Layer 2). Assigned on first insert; coalesced by dedup worker. HIN is gold-standard dedup signal. Added v1.7.0.

columns.name: vin
columns.notes: Nullable — older/used boats often lack VIN; deduplication uses make+model+year+hours+price-tolerance

columns.name: current_hours
columns.notes: Updated as new listing data arrives

columns.name: condition
columns.notes: Excellent | Good | Fair

columns.name: actual_engine_hp
columns.notes: Actual HP if known and differs from model base; null = assume base model spec

columns.name: has_tower
columns.notes: Actual tower presence on this unit; null = unknown

columns.name: actual_ballast_lbs
columns.notes: Actual ballast on this unit; null = assume base model spec

columns.name: dealer_key
columns.notes: Last known dealer association

dimensions.description: Competitor brands for Phase 2 competitive overlay analytics.

facts.description: Core fact table. One row per listing per day. Backbone of pricing, time-on-market, and inventory level analytics.
facts.grain: one row per listing per day

columns.name: listing_id
columns.notes: Source system listing identifier

columns.name: original_list_price
columns.notes: First observed price for this listing

columns.name: price_change_amount
columns.notes: Delta from prior day snapshot

columns.name: days_on_market
columns.notes: Computed from first_seen_date

columns.name: listing_status
columns.notes: Active | Removed | Price Reduced

columns.name: is_new_listing
columns.notes: True on first day seen

columns.name: is_removed
columns.notes: True on last day seen — inferred sold or delisted

facts.description: One row per inferred sale event. A listing removed after active status is treated as a likely sale. confidence_score allows downstream analytics to discount low-confidence records.
facts.grain: one row per inferred sale event

columns.name: confidence_score
columns.notes: 0.000–1.000; sale vs. delisting confidence

columns.name: sale_type
columns.notes: New | Used

facts.description: Impressions, clicks, and leads for listings actively hosted on the Wakr platform. Powers Dealer Performance Indicators, Model-Level Impressions, and Lead Activity Tracking.
facts.grain: one row per listing per day

columns.name: click_through_rate
columns.notes: Computed: clicks / impressions

facts.description: Activity from wakr.co DTC members — boat ownership, lake visits, lessons, dealer touchpoints.
facts.grain: one row per member activity event

columns.name: activity_type
columns.notes: Ride | Lesson | Service | Dealer Visit

facts.description: Browsing, comparison, and purchase events on the Wakr marketplace. Enables demand shift detection before inventory data reflects it. Anonymous sessions have no member_key.

columns.name: event_type
columns.notes: View | Compare | Save | Mention | Purchase

bridges.description: Many-to-many affiliation between dealer locations and manufacturers/brands. A dealer may be an authorized dealer for multiple brands (e.g. Malibu + Axis + Centurion). The is_primary flag identifies the dealer's primary brand for convenience denormalization. Use this table for any multi-brand dealer queries; the primary_manufacturer_key FK on dim_dealer is a denormalized shortcut for single-brand lookups.

columns.name: is_primary
columns.notes: True for the dealer's dominant brand

columns.name: is_authorized
columns.notes: Manufacturer-confirmed authorized dealer status

columns.name: authorization_type
columns.notes: CHECK (Layer 2 values): full | service_only | parts_only | pending | other. CORRECTED v1.7.0 — prior ERD had Full | Limited | Service-Only.

bridges.description: Multi-valued listing features extracted from scrape text (e.g. tower type, ballast configuration, electronics). One listing → many feature rows. Used for comparable listing matching in the Trade-In & Valuation Application.

columns.name: feature_category
columns.notes: Maps to listing_features.category (Layer 2). CHECK values: tower | ballast | electronics | upholstery | engine_upgrade | trailer | other. CORRECTED v1.7.0 — 'trailer' was missing.

marts.description: Central analytics workhorse mart. One row per manufacturer / boat_model / state / inventory_type / day. Every dashboard metric is a column on this row. All parameter combinations (make, model, state, inventory_type, time range) are resolved at query time via WHERE and GROUP BY — no permutations are pre-stored. Refreshed nightly to keep trailing window metrics current relative to the user's view date. Also refreshed on scrape completion when new source data arrives.
marts.time_range_note: All trailing windows resolved at query time: WHERE date_key >= CURRENT_DATE - N. mart_daily_snapshot is the sole source for all trailing window queries across all dashboard tabs.
marts.stock_vs_flow_note: STOCK columns are point-in-time (do NOT SUM across days — use latest row or AVG). FLOW columns are daily event counts (safe to SUM across any window). See schema.query_layer_notes for full rules.

columns.name: date_key
columns.notes: Calendar date this row represents.

columns.name: boat_model_key
columns.notes: NULL row included per manufacturer to support Make-only (all models) queries without a separate rollup pass.

columns.name: state
columns.notes: State abbreviation. NULL row included to support all-states rollup.

columns.name: inventory_type
columns.notes: New | Used. Combined is resolved at query time by omitting the inventory_type filter — not stored as a third value to avoid double-counting.

columns.name: active_listings
columns.notes: Count of listings with listing_status = Active as of this date. Do NOT SUM across days — use latest date in window for current count, or AVG for period average.

columns.name: avg_list_price
columns.notes: Average listed_price for active listings on this date. Do NOT SUM across days — use AVG(avg_list_price) weighted by active_listings when rolling up across days.

columns.name: median_list_price
columns.notes: Median listed_price for active listings on this date. Approximated at daily aggregation. Do NOT SUM across days.

columns.name: avg_dom
columns.notes: Average days_on_market for active listings on this date. Do NOT SUM — use AVG(avg_dom) weighted by active_listings when rolling up.

columns.name: dom_bucket_0_7
columns.notes: Active listings with DOM 0-7 days on this date. Do NOT SUM across days.

columns.name: dom_bucket_8_15
columns.notes: Active listings with DOM 8-15 days on this date. Do NOT SUM across days.

columns.name: dom_bucket_16_30
columns.notes: Active listings with DOM 16-30 days on this date. Do NOT SUM across days.

columns.name: dom_bucket_31_60
columns.notes: Active listings with DOM 31-60 days on this date. Do NOT SUM across days.

columns.name: dom_bucket_60_plus
columns.notes: Active listings with DOM 60+ days on this date. Do NOT SUM across days.

columns.name: min_list_price
columns.notes: Minimum listed_price among active listings on this date. Supports price band floor derivation.

columns.name: max_list_price
columns.notes: Maximum listed_price among active listings on this date. Supports price band ceiling derivation.

columns.name: listing_count_band_under_60k
columns.notes: Active listings priced under $60k on this date.

columns.name: listing_count_band_60_80k
columns.notes: Active listings priced $60k-$80k on this date.

columns.name: listing_count_band_80_100k
columns.notes: Active listings priced $80k-$100k on this date.

columns.name: listing_count_band_100_120k
columns.notes: Active listings priced $100k-$120k on this date.

columns.name: listing_count_band_120_140k
columns.notes: Active listings priced $120k-$140k on this date.

columns.name: listing_count_band_over_140k
columns.notes: Active listings priced over $140k on this date.

columns.name: avg_dom_band_under_60k
columns.notes: Avg DOM for under $60k active listings on this date.

columns.name: avg_dom_band_60_80k
columns.notes: Avg DOM for $60k-$80k active listings on this date.

columns.name: avg_dom_band_80_100k
columns.notes: Avg DOM for $80k-$100k active listings on this date.

columns.name: avg_dom_band_100_120k
columns.notes: Avg DOM for $100k-$120k active listings on this date.

columns.name: avg_dom_band_120_140k
columns.notes: Avg DOM for $120k-$140k active listings on this date.

columns.name: avg_dom_band_over_140k
columns.notes: Avg DOM for over $140k active listings on this date.

columns.name: new_listings
columns.notes: Count of listings where is_new_listing = true on this date. Safe to SUM across any window. Drives Inventory Added (T30) KPI.

columns.name: removed_listings
columns.notes: Count of listings where is_removed = true on this date. Proxy for boats sold. Safe to SUM across any window. Drives Boats Sold KPI.

columns.name: price_reduced_listings
columns.notes: Count of listings where price_change_amount < 0 on this date. Safe to SUM — but deduplicate by listing_id across window to avoid counting the same listing multiple times for pct_listings_with_price_cut.

columns.name: dom_status
columns.notes: Velocity classification for this row: Fast (<15d avg_dom) | Healthy (15-22d) | Slow (23-30d) | Very Slow (30d+). Pre-classified at mart refresh. Use latest date in window.

columns.name: sell_through_rate
columns.notes: removed_listings / active_listings for this date. Pre-computed for convenience. For window roll-up: SUM(removed_listings) / AVG(active_listings) across window.

columns.name: days_supply
columns.notes: active_listings / (removed_listings_t30 / 30.0). Requires a 30-day trailing window regardless of selected time range. Always computed against T30 removed_listings.

columns.name: is_partial_scrape_day
columns.notes: TRUE if this date falls within an active scrape window and data may be incomplete. Query layer should exclude or flag partial days for flow metrics.

columns.name: last_scrape_date
columns.notes: Date of the most recent scrape that contributed data to this row. Allows query layer to surface data freshness to the user.

indexes_recommended: PRIMARY KEY (manufacturer_key, boat_model_key, state, inventory_type, date_key)
INDEX (date_key) — for trailing window date range scans
INDEX (manufacturer_key, date_key) — for make-level all-models queries
INDEX (state, date_key) — for regional tab all-makes queries

marts.description: Pre-aggregated active listing counts, inventory turnover, and days-on-market distribution by make/model/region/week. Powers the Active Listings Monitor, Days on Market Distribution chart, Total Inventory Trend line chart, and Inventory & Velocity table on the Inventory tab. Grain expanded in v1.5.0 to include boat_model_key, enabling Model filter on the Inventory tab. v1.8.0: Trailing window metrics removed — all served by mart_daily_snapshot at query time. This mart retains monthly-grain metrics only: those requiring calendar-month alignment (MoM comparisons, momentum_status classification, vs_market_pct). Refreshed on scrape-triggered schedule only.
marts.grain: make / model / state / month (monthly pre-agg); use mart_daily_snapshot for sub-monthly windows
dashboard_widgets: KPI: Active Listings
KPI: Boats Sold (30D)
KPI: Inventory Added (T30)
KPI: Avg Days on Market
Chart: Days on Market Distribution (bar, 5 buckets)
Chart: Total Inventory Trend (line, active listings over time)
Table: Inventory & Velocity (Model-Year / Avg DOM / Active Units / Boats Sold T30 / Momentum)

computed_columns.name: sell_through_rate
computed_columns.notes: units_sold / active_listings for the period. Demand defined as boats sold count.

computed_columns.name: mom_listing_change_pct
computed_columns.notes: Month-over-month % change in active listing count. Drives the MoM Change KPI on Inventory tab.

computed_columns.name: days_supply
computed_columns.notes: active_listings / (boats_sold_t30 / 30.0). Displayed per state on the Regional tab Days Supply column.

computed_columns.name: momentum_status
computed_columns.notes: RESOLVED (v1.5.0): Accelerating = avg_dom trending down MoM AND sell_through_rate trending up; Slowing = avg_dom trending up MoM OR sell_through_rate trending down; Stable = neither threshold met. Drives the Momentum column in the Inventory & Velocity table.

computed_columns.name: aging_risk_level
computed_columns.notes: Aging risk classification: Elevated | Moderate | Low. Derived from avg_dom vs configurable thresholds.

supported_time_ranges: monthly grain only — trailing windows via mart_daily_snapshot
marts.time_range_notes: T7 and T30 sourced from mart_daily_snapshot. T90, YTD, L12M computed by aggregating mart_daily_snapshot rows or rolling up monthly mart rows. boats_sold_t30, inventory_added_t30, and avg_dom computed columns are defined for T30; equivalent _t7, _t90, _ytd, _l12m variants are resolved at query time from mart_daily_snapshot rather than stored as separate columns to avoid column explosion.
marts.trailing_window_note: Trailing window metrics removed v1.8.0 — served by mart_daily_snapshot. active_listings, inventory_added_t30, boats_sold_t30, avg_dom, dom_bucket_* moved to mart_daily_snapshot.

marts.description: NEW in v1.5.0. State-level market rollup supporting the Regional tab. One row per state / make / model / month. Powers the four KPI boxes (Fastest Market, Slowest Market, Top Growth State, Sales Trends), the State Market Map choropleth, State Market Leaders top/bottom rankings, and the State Market Overview detail table. Grain includes boat_model_key to support the Model filter parameter added in v1.5.0. v1.8.0: Trailing window metrics removed — all served by mart_daily_snapshot at query time. This mart retains monthly-grain metrics only: those requiring calendar-month alignment (MoM comparisons, momentum_status classification, vs_market_pct). Refreshed on scrape-triggered schedule only.
dashboard_widgets: KPI: Fastest Market (state abbreviation + avg DOM + % vs national avg)
KPI: Slowest Market (state abbreviation + avg DOM + % vs national avg)
KPI: Top Growth State (state abbreviation + YoY supply % change)
KPI: Sales Trends (count of states with rising vs falling sales)
Widget: State Market Map (choropleth, metric selector: Velocity/Avg DOM/Listings)
Widget: State Market Leaders — Top States by Boats Sold (rank, state, units, listing count)
Widget: State Market Leaders — Lowest Sales States (rank, state, units, listing count)
Table: State Market Overview (State, Listings, Avg DOM, Boats Sold T30, % Market, Average Price)

computed_columns.name: avg_dom_vs_national_pct
computed_columns.notes: ((state_avg_dom - national_avg_dom) / national_avg_dom). Negative = faster than national avg (displayed as '35% faster than national avg' on Fastest Market KPI). Positive = slower (displayed as '48% above national avg' on Slowest Market KPI).

computed_columns.name: boats_sold_t30_vs_market_pct
computed_columns.notes: Comparison % of state's boats_sold_t30 vs all-states average for the selected make/model. Drives comparison indicator on the Sales Trends KPI.

computed_columns.name: pct_market
computed_columns.notes: State's share of total national listings for the selected make/model/period. Drives the % Market column in State Market Overview (e.g. FL = 15.2%).

computed_columns.name: avg_price_vs_market_pct
computed_columns.notes: Comparison % of state avg_price vs national average price for the selected make/model. Drives comparison indicator on avg_price.

computed_columns.name: yoy_supply_change_pct
computed_columns.notes: Year-over-year % change in listing count for the state. Drives the Top Growth State KPI box value (e.g. TX +9.1%).

computed_columns.name: dom_status
computed_columns.notes: Velocity classification for map color-coding: Fast (<15d) | Healthy (15–22d) | Slow (23–30d) | Very Slow (30d+). Applied per state row.

computed_columns.name: sales_trend_direction
computed_columns.notes: Rising | Falling | Stable. MoM direction of boats_sold_t30 for the state. Used to compute the Sales Trends KPI count (e.g. 8 rising / 10 falling).

computed_columns.name: fastest_market_state
computed_columns.notes: Derived/flagged column identifying the state with the lowest avg_dom in the period for the selected make/model. Drives the Fastest Market KPI. Computed at mart refresh.

computed_columns.name: slowest_market_state
computed_columns.notes: Derived/flagged column identifying the state with the highest avg_dom in the period for the selected make/model. Drives the Slowest Market KPI. Computed at mart refresh.

supported_time_ranges: monthly grain only — trailing windows via mart_daily_snapshot
marts.time_range_notes: T7 not supported — regional state-level aggregations require sufficient sample size; minimum window is T30. T90, YTD, L12M computed by rolling up monthly rows. yoy_supply_change_pct always compares the equivalent period last year regardless of selected window.
marts.trailing_window_note: Trailing window metrics removed v1.8.0 — served by mart_daily_snapshot. listings, avg_dom, boats_sold_t30, avg_price moved to mart_daily_snapshot.

marts.description: Pre-aggregated advertised price distributions, median prices, price movement, and price-band breakdowns by make/model/region/month. Powers the Pricing tab KPI boxes, Avg Days on Market by Price Tier chart, Listings by Price Tier chart, and Model Price Efficiency table. Grain includes boat_model_key to support Model filter added in v1.5.0. v1.8.0: Trailing window metrics removed — all served by mart_daily_snapshot at query time. This mart retains monthly-grain metrics only: those requiring calendar-month alignment (MoM comparisons, momentum_status classification, vs_market_pct). Refreshed on scrape-triggered schedule only.
dashboard_widgets: KPI: Avg List Price
KPI: Median Price
KPI: Month over Month Price Change
KPI: Top Selling Band (price range label + units sold)
Chart: Avg Days on Market by Price Tier (bar, 6 price bands, color-coded by DOM status)
Chart: Listings by Price Tier (horizontal bar, 6 price bands, unit counts)
Table: Model Price Efficiency (Model-Year, Avg List Price, Price Band, Avg DOM, Listings count)

computed_columns.name: avg_list_price
computed_columns.notes: Average listed_price for active listings in the period. Drives the Avg List Price KPI box.

computed_columns.name: avg_list_price_vs_market_pct
computed_columns.notes: Comparison % of user company's avg_list_price vs all-makes market average. Drives comparison indicator on Avg List Price KPI box.

computed_columns.name: median_list_price
computed_columns.notes: Median listed_price for active listings in the period. Mid-market indicator. Drives the Median Price KPI box.

computed_columns.name: median_list_price_vs_market_pct
computed_columns.notes: Comparison % of user company's median_list_price vs all-makes market average. Drives comparison indicator on Median Price KPI box.

computed_columns.name: mom_price_change_pct
computed_columns.notes: Month-over-month % change in avg_list_price vs prior month. Drives the Month over Month Price Change KPI box (e.g. -4.1%).

computed_columns.name: mom_price_change_pct_vs_market_pct
computed_columns.notes: Comparison % of user company's mom_price_change_pct vs all-makes market average rate of change. Drives comparison indicator on MoM Price Change KPI box.

computed_columns.name: top_selling_band_low
computed_columns.notes: Lower bound of the price band with the highest boats_sold_t30 count. Together with top_selling_band_high, drives the Top Selling Band KPI label (e.g. '$140–160k').

computed_columns.name: top_selling_band_high
computed_columns.notes: Upper bound of the top-selling price band. NULL if the band is open-ended (e.g. Over $140k).

computed_columns.name: top_selling_band_units
computed_columns.notes: Count of units sold in the top selling band for the period. Drives the sub-label on the Top Selling Band KPI (e.g. '134 units sold this month').

computed_columns.name: top_selling_band_vs_market_pct
computed_columns.notes: Comparison % indicating whether user company's top selling band volume is above or below market average for that band. Drives comparison indicator on Top Selling Band KPI box.

computed_columns.name: price_band_label
computed_columns.notes: Display label for the model's primary price band (e.g. '$73k–$85k'). Drives the Price Band column in the Model Price Efficiency table. Derived from min/max of listed_price for the model in the period.

computed_columns.name: pct_listings_with_price_cut
computed_columns.notes: Fraction of active listings where price_change_amount < 0 (or listing_status = 'Price Reduced'). Drives the 'Listings w/ Price Cut' KPI on Pricing tab.

computed_columns.name: discount_pressure_pct
computed_columns.notes: avg((original_list_price - listed_price) / original_list_price) for listings with price cuts. Drives the Discount Pressure column on Model Price Efficiency table.

supported_time_ranges: monthly grain only — trailing windows via mart_daily_snapshot
marts.time_range_notes: T7 not supported at pricing mart level — price distributions require sufficient sample size; minimum window is T30. T90, YTD, L12M computed by averaging/aggregating the appropriate monthly rows. mom_price_change_pct always refers to the most recent calendar month vs prior month regardless of selected window.
marts.trailing_window_note: Trailing window metrics removed v1.8.0 — served by mart_daily_snapshot. avg_dom_by_price_band_* and listing_count_by_price_band_* moved to mart_daily_snapshot.

marts.description: Average and percentile days-on-market by make/model/region/month with aging distribution buckets. v1.8.0: Trailing window metrics removed — all served by mart_daily_snapshot at query time. This mart retains monthly-grain metrics only: those requiring calendar-month alignment (MoM comparisons, momentum_status classification, vs_market_pct). Refreshed on scrape-triggered schedule only.

computed_columns.name: dom_status
computed_columns.notes: Velocity-speed classification based on avg_dom thresholds: Fast (<15d) | Healthy (15-22d) | Slow (23-30d) | Very Slow (30d+). Drives the Pricing tab legend and color indicators across all tabs.

computed_columns.name: aging_risk_level
computed_columns.notes: Aging risk classification for Inventory tab Aging Risk column: Elevated | Moderate | Low. Derived from avg_dom vs configurable thresholds.

supported_time_ranges: monthly grain only — trailing windows via mart_daily_snapshot
marts.time_range_notes: T7 sourced from mart_daily_snapshot avg_dom. All other windows computed from monthly mart rows or daily snapshot rollups.
marts.trailing_window_note: Trailing window metrics removed v1.8.0 — served by mart_daily_snapshot. avg_dom moved to mart_daily_snapshot.

marts.description: Dealer-location and dealer-network performance metrics benchmarked against network averages: inventory count, avg days-on-market, leads, impressions, estimated sell-through. Supports both individual location view and rolled-up dealer_network_key grouping. Dealers tab not yet implemented — mart retained for Phase 1 readiness.

computed_columns.name: delta_week_units
computed_columns.notes: Week-over-week change in units sold for the dealer. Requires weekly sub-aggregation from fact_listing_snapshot / fact_estimated_sale. Drives the Delta Week column on Dealers tab.

computed_columns.name: market_share_pct
computed_columns.notes: PENDING USER RESEARCH: Dealer network's share of total market listings per state. Methodology (numerator/denominator definition) to be confirmed before implementation. Drives Market Share by State chart on Dealers tab.

marts.description: Estimated sell-through counts and pace by make/model/region/month. Powers velocity rankings including Demand-Supply Ratio, Momentum, and Avg DOM trend. v1.8.0: Trailing window metrics removed — all served by mart_daily_snapshot at query time. This mart retains monthly-grain metrics only: those requiring calendar-month alignment (MoM comparisons, momentum_status classification, vs_market_pct). Refreshed on scrape-triggered schedule only.

computed_columns.name: demand_supply_ratio
computed_columns.notes: boats_sold_t30 / active_listings. Demand is defined as count of boats sold over the measurement period. Drives the Demand-Supply Ratio column on the Velocity tab.

computed_columns.name: momentum_status
computed_columns.notes: RESOLVED (v1.5.0): Accelerating | Stable | Slowing. See schema notes for definition. Drives the Momentum column on Inventory & Velocity table and Velocity tab.

supported_time_ranges: monthly grain only — trailing windows via mart_daily_snapshot
marts.time_range_notes: T7 not supported — velocity/momentum classifications require sufficient sample size; minimum window is T30. demand_supply_ratio denominator (boats_sold) is always a trailing 30-day count regardless of selected window to maintain comparability; the window filter controls which listing snapshots are included in the active_listings numerator.
marts.trailing_window_note: Trailing window metrics removed v1.8.0 — served by mart_daily_snapshot. trailing window velocity metrics moved to mart_daily_snapshot.

marts.description: Boat model usage patterns by lake and region. Surfaces which models are actively ridden in specific areas.

relationships.notes: Denormalized primary brand only; full list via bridge_dealer_manufacturer
Join on state CHAR(2) for full state name display in Regional tab table.
Downstream projection of gear_brands. Sync daily.
Downstream projection of boats_models. Sync daily.
Downstream projection of waters. Sync daily.
Downstream projection of dealer_groups. Sync daily.
Downstream projection of dealers. Sync daily.
dealers.water_id links dealer location to nearest lake. Resolved via dim_lake in Data Lake.
